sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Mithril Protocol continuous development through Intersect on behalf of the vendor Teragone.
Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v
Deliverables: Mithril Protocol Continuous Development, Intersect budget administration and oversight, Milestone-based disbursement controls through TRSC and PSSC smart contracts

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Mithril addresses slow full-node synchronization and centralized-trust light-client access by using stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says faster sync times would lower onboarding friction and unlock tooling for exchanges, wallets, and monitoring.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal states Mithril enables community-built bridges, light wallets, full wallets, light clients, and monitoring tools around Cardano.
Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The requested withdrawal is stated not to breach the applicable 350M Net Change Limit for Epoch 613 to Epoch 713 at the moment of submission.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The budget allocates 3,699,440 ADA to Mithril Protocol Continuous Development and 110,983 ADA to the Intersect Budget Administration fee.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says faster sync times would lower onboarding friction and unlock tooling for exchanges, wallets, and monitoring.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states Mithril enables community-built bridges, light wallets, full wallets, light clients, and monitoring tools around Cardano.
Independent evidence for: Audit and oversight costs are included in the proposal overhead and administration fee, with independent oversight through Intersect and technically capable third parties.
Independent evidence for: Teragone has not previously received direct funding from Cardano treasury or community governance programs but has worked on Mithril through IOG-funded collaboration.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Mithril addresses slow full-node synchronization and centralized-trust light-client access by using stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says faster sync times would lower onboarding friction and unlock tooling for exchanges, wallets, and monitoring." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
